title: Vonage Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Vonage** (formerly Nexmo) is a CPaaS platform exposing a federated REST API for SMS, Voice, Verify (2FA), Numbers, Messages, and Conversations. This connector — `VonageConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "vonage"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base URL | Auth | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|
| Account | `https://rest.nexmo.com` | api_key + secret | `get_balance` — credential probe |
| SMS | `https://rest.nexmo.com/sms/json` | api_key + secret (form) | `send_sms`, `get_sms_status`, `list_messages` |
| Numbers | `https://rest.nexmo.com/account/numbers`, `/number/*` | api_key + secret | `list_numbers`, `search_numbers`, `buy_number`, `cancel_number` |
| Verify v2 | `https://api.nexmo.com/v2/verify` | api_key + secret | `send_verify_request`, `check_verify_code`, `cancel_verify` |
| Voice | `https://api.nexmo.com/v1/calls` | RS256 JWT + application_id | `create_call`, `get_call`, `list_calls`, `update_call`, `get_call_recording` |
| Applications | `https://api.nexmo.com/v2/applications` | api_key + secret | `list_applications` |

The connector normalises SMS + Calls into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts), honours `Retry-After` on 429, parses Vonage's SMS / Verify HTTP-200 envelopes for non-zero `status` codes, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::VonageHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Get your Vonage credentials

1. Sign in to https://dashboard.nexmo.com.
2. From **Getting Started** copy your **API Key** and **API Secret** (top of the page).
3. *(Optional — Voice / Messages only)* In **Applications → Create a new application**:
   - Enable the **Voice** (or Messages) capability.
   - Click **Generate public/private key pair** — Vonage downloads a `private.key` PEM file. Save it.
   - Click **Create application** — copy the generated **Application ID** (UUID).
4. *(Optional — webhook verification)* In **Settings → Signed webhooks** copy the **webhook signing secret**.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Vonage**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (text) | From step 2 |
| `api_secret` (secret) | From step 2 |
| `application_id` (text, optional) | From step 3 — needed only for Voice / Messages |
| `private_key` (textarea, optional) | Paste the entire PEM from step 3 |
| `webhook_secret` (secret, optional) | From step 4 — verifies inbound JWTs |
| `timeout_s` (number) | Per-request timeout, default 30 |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft advisory, default 30 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_key` + `api_secret` (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `VonageHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /account/get-balance`. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning. On 402 (insufficient funds) → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with the balance message.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Vonage is **federated**: different surfaces require different credentials. The connector accepts both at install time and routes each call to the right authenticator.

### Mode A — API key + secret (default)

Used for: SMS, Account, Numbers, Verify, Applications, Search.

- Sent as **HTTP Basic** header on every request:
  ```
  Authorization: Basic base64(api_key:api_secret)
  ```
- Legacy SMS / Verify endpoints **also** accept `api_key` and `api_secret` as form fields — the client sends both Basic header AND form fields so any internal routing path inside Vonage still authenticates.

### Mode B — RS256 JWT + application_id

Used for: Voice (`/v1/calls`), Messages, Conversations.

- The connector mints a short-lived JWT (60s TTL) per request:
  ```json
  {
    "iat": <now>,
    "jti": <uuid4>,
    "exp": <now+60>,
    "application_id": <self.application_id>
  }
  ```
  signed with `RS256` using the user-supplied PEM private key.
- Sent as `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`.
- This mode is **optional** at install — `application_id` + `private_key` are not in `REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS`. Voice methods raise `VonageConfigError` if the caller tries to use them without the JWT credentials.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key` + `api_secret`. Does **not** call the API. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /account/get-balance` — minimal probe. |
| `sync()` | Aggregates recent SMS + Calls. Skips calls iteration if JWT credentials are absent. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — api_key has no expiry; JWT is re-minted per request. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract (Mode A)

```
Authorization: Basic base64(api_key:api_secret)
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json     ← only when sending JSON body
```

### Header contract (Mode B)

```
Authorization: Bearer <RS256 JWT>
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json     ← only when sending JSON body
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | text | yes | `self.api_key` | HTTP Basic username |
| `api_secret` | secret | yes | `self.api_secret` | HTTP Basic password |
| `application_id` | text | no | `self.application_id` | JWT `application_id` claim — required for Voice |
| `private_key` | textarea | no | `self.private_key` | RSA PEM used to sign RS256 JWTs |
| `webhook_secret` | secret | no | `self.config["webhook_secret"]` | HS256 secret used to verify inbound JWT callbacks |
| `timeout_s` | number | no | `self.config["timeout_s"]` (default 30) | Per-request timeout |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | advisory only | Soft client-side cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key", "api_secret"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
REST_BASE_URL = "https://rest.nexmo.com"
API_BASE_URL  = "https://api.nexmo.com"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `VonageConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::VonageHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key` + `api_secret`; initialise `VonageHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /account/get-balance`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates recent SMS via `/search/messages` → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_batch`. If JWT credentials are configured, also iterates Voice calls via `/v1/calls`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: SMS
children.content:
### `async send_sms(payload: Dict) -> Dict`
`POST /sms/json` — send a text/unicode SMS. Payload at minimum: `{from, to, text}`. The HTTP client merges `api_key` + `api_secret` into the form body. Envelope-checked: a non-zero `messages[].status` raises `VonageInsufficientFunds` (status 9), `VonageAuthError` (status 4/14), or `VonageError`.

### `async get_sms_status(message_id: str) -> Dict`
`GET /search/message?id=<message_id>` — look up the state of a previously-sent SMS.

### `async list_messages(*, date=None, to=None, page_index=None, page_size=100) -> Dict`
`GET /search/messages` — paginated. Returns `{items, next_url, count}`. `next_url` is `_links.next.href`; when absent, the caller has reached the last page.

children.title: Voice (JWT mode)
children.content:
### `async create_call(payload: Dict) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/calls`. Payload must include `to` and exactly one of `ncco` or `answer_url`. Raises `ValueError` if neither is present. Requires `application_id` + `private_key`.

### `async get_call(call_uuid: str) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/calls/{uuid}`.

### `async update_call(call_uuid: str, payload: Dict) -> Dict`
`PUT /v1/calls/{uuid}` — change call state (e.g. `{action: "hangup"}`).

### `async list_calls(*, status=None, date_start=None, date_end=None, page_size=10, record_index=0) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/calls` — paginated. Returns `{items, next_url, count}`. Items come from `_embedded.calls`; next page index from `_links.next.href`.

### `async get_call_recording(recording_url: str) -> bytes`
`GET <recording_url>` — download raw audio bytes. The URL is delivered by the `recording:available` event.

children.title: Verify v2
children.content:
### `async send_verify_request(payload: Dict) -> Dict`
`POST /v2/verify` — start a verification. Body: `{brand, workflow: [{channel, to, from?}], code_length?, ...}`. Returns `{request_id}`.

### `async check_verify_code(request_id: str, code: str) -> Dict`
`POST /v2/verify/{request_id}` — submit the OTP the user entered.

### `async cancel_verify(request_id: str) -> Dict`
`DELETE /v2/verify/{request_id}` — abort an in-flight verification.

children.title: Numbers
children.content:
### `async list_numbers(*, country=None, pattern=None, search_pattern=0, features=None, size=10, index=1) -> Dict`
`GET /account/numbers` — numbers currently owned by the account.

### `async search_numbers(country, *, pattern=None, type_="mobile-lvn", features="SMS,VOICE", size=10) -> Dict`
`GET /number/search` — numbers available to purchase.

### `async buy_number(country, msisdn) -> Dict`
`POST /number/buy` — provision a number into this account.

### `async cancel_number(country, msisdn) -> Dict`
`POST /number/cancel` — release a number from this account.

children.title: Applications
children.content:
### `async list_applications(*, page_size=10, page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /v2/applications` — list Vonage Applications for this account. Items from `_embedded.applications`.

children.title: Webhooks
children.content:
### `async handle_webhook(payload, headers=None) -> Dict`
Verifies the signature (if `webhook_secret` is set) then routes by `event_type`:
- `message:submitted` / `message:delivered` / `message:rejected`
- `call:started` / `call:answered` / `call:completed` / `recording:available`

Voice payloads that lack `event_type` are bucketed by their `status` field (e.g. `status="answered"` → `call:answered`).

Unknown events return `{status: "ignored", event_type}`.

### `async process_callback(payload, headers=None) -> Dict`
Verifies the Vonage callback signature:
1. **Primary**: HS256 JWT in `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` (Vonage's modern signed-webhook scheme). Verified against `self.config["webhook_secret"]`.
2. **Fallback**: HMAC-SHA256 hex digest in `X-Vonage-Signature` over the canonical JSON body.

Returns `{verified, data?, error?}`. If `webhook_secret` is empty, returns `{verified: True, unverified: True}` to allow dev-mode usage.

### `async handle_event(event) -> Dict`
Idempotent event acknowledgement keyed by `message_uuid` / `uuid` / `id`.

### `async batch_processor(items) -> Dict`
Iterate events through `handle_event` with per-item error capture.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
VonageError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── VonageAuthError                    # 401 / 403, envelope status 4 / 14
├── VonageBadRequestError              # 400
├── VonageNotFoundError                # 404
├── VonageConflictError                # 409
├── VonageRateLimitError               # 429 — retry_after_s
├── VonageServerError                  # 5xx — retry candidate
├── VonageInsufficientFunds            # 402 / envelope status 9
└── VonageConfigError                  # JWT mode required but private_key missing
```

Back-compat alias preserved:
```
VonageNetworkError = VonageServerError    # legacy name from older code
```

### Envelope errors (HTTP 200 with non-zero `status`)

SMS `/sms/json` and Verify `/verify/*` can return `HTTP 200` with a `messages[].status` (SMS) or top-level `status` (Verify) that is **not** `"0"`. The HTTP client parses these envelopes and raises:

| Envelope `status` | Exception |
|---|---|
| `"9"` | `VonageInsufficientFunds` |
| `"4"` or `"14"` | `VonageAuthError` |
| other non-zero | `VonageError` |

Voice payloads with `status` in `{"started", "answered", "ringing", "completed"}` are treated as success and not flagged.

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 / 402 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` header; sleep then retry up to `max_retries=3` |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff `min(2 ** attempt, 8)`s, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then propagate |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_classify_failure`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
402 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   — message includes balance
5xx → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
VonageConfigError → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)
other → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, FAILED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` or `api_secret` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Vonage Dashboard → Getting Started shows current values. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Vonage — credentials valid but lack scope | Verify the account is fully provisioned (no pending billing / verification on Vonage Dashboard). |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — bad `api_key` / `api_secret` | Rotate the secret in Vonage Dashboard → Getting Started → "Reset API Secret", then re-install. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)` with `insufficient funds` message | Account balance < message cost | Top up at Vonage Dashboard → Billing. The connector keeps serving non-paid endpoints. |
| `VonageConfigError: Voice / Messages calls require both application_id and private_key` | Caller used `create_call` / `get_call` etc. without JWT creds at install | Re-install with `application_id` + the PEM `private_key` from the matching Vonage Application. |
| `vonage.http.rate_limited` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Vonage per-second SMS cap) | The client retries with `Retry-After` backoff. Reduce send rate or throttle on the caller side. |
| `VonageInsufficientFunds` from `send_sms` even with positive balance | SMS envelope `status: "9"` — destination country requires sender registration | Verify the destination country supports the sender ID; for India/US numbers, use a registered long code rather than alphanumeric. |
| `process_callback` returns `{verified: False, error: "jwt_invalid: ..."}` | `webhook_secret` doesn't match the Dashboard value | Copy the secret from Vonage Dashboard → Settings → Signed webhooks (case-sensitive) and re-install. |
| `process_callback` returns `{verified: False, error: "signature_missing"}` | Callback arrived with no `Authorization` header AND no `X-Vonage-Signature` | Enable webhook signing in Vonage Dashboard so callbacks carry the HS256 JWT; or remove `webhook_secret` from install to accept unsigned callbacks (dev only). |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'VonageNetworkError'` | Older callsite using pre-rewrite exception name | The alias `VonageNetworkError = VonageServerError` is preserved in `exceptions.py` — no code change needed. |
| Voice call payload accepted by Vonage but no audio plays | NCCO referenced an unreachable `answer_url` | The Vonage event stream will deliver `call:completed` with a reason — inspect via `handle_webhook` event handlers. Verify the URL is publicly reachable + returns valid NCCO JSON. |
